L1.area: Server-side API controllers and data processing logic

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where user-controlled input from HTTP POST requests is passed directly to a PHP deserialization function without restricting the classes that can be instantiated. An unauthenticated remote attacker can craft a malicious serialized payload to achieve arbitrary code execution on the server.

L3.area: API controllers responsible for application update synchronization and third-party messaging platform webhook processing
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where user-controlled input from HTTP POST requests is passed directly to a PHP deserialization function without restricting allowed classes. The vulnerability affects controllers that process application file update rules and third-party messaging platform event data, enabling an unauthenticated remote attacker to achieve arbitrary code execution via crafted serialized payloads.

ground_truth.reason: User-controlled POST parameters are passed directly to PHP's unserialize() function without restricting allowed classes, enabling an attacker to instantiate arbitrary objects and achieve remote code execution via crafted serialized payloads.
